title: acilepis heynei
intro: Acilepis heynei, also known as the 'Tulsi of the Hills', is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to the Himalayan region and Southeast Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which contribute to its health benefits. The leaves of the Acilepis heynei plant have been traditionally used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams to alleviate symptoms of fever, cold, and respiratory issues, as well as to promote wound healing and skin health. In addition, the plant's extracts are being studied for their potential to inhibit microbial growth and exhibit immunomodulatory effects, highlighting its promise as a natural remedy for various health conditions.
